name: Galaxy Half-Light Radius Measurement
description:
Determine the effective (half-light) radius of the spiral galaxy in ~/AstroImages/raw/uit_galaxy_sample.fits using curve-of-growth analysis. Calculate the sky background mean from a 50x50 pixel region at the top-left corner (0,0) and identify the galaxy's central core coordinates (X, Y) at the point of maximum brightness. Determine the total background-subtracted flux within a 120-pixel radius centered on the core, then find the integer radius containing 50% of that flux. Save the results to ~/AstroImages/measurements/half_light_radius.txt using the following format:

# Galaxy Morphology Report
# Image: uit_galaxy_sample.fits

sky_background_mean: <value>
center_x: <value>
center_y: <value>
total_flux_r120: <value>
half_light_radius: <value>
